title: 专业精神 / Professionalism
summary: 本章界定专业人士的四大特征、构成一项专业的基本要求、客户对专业行为的十项期望,以及财务顾问代表作为专业人士的责任。结论是:FAA 下的财务咨询业满足专业的各项条件,代表应以专业人士而非单纯推销员的标准行事,并把客户最佳利益置于自身利益之前。 / This chapter defines the four characteristics of a professional, the basic requirements for a profession, the ten client expectations of professional behaviour, and the responsibilities of a financial adviser representative as a professional. It concludes that the financial advisory business under the FAA qualifies as a profession, and that representatives must act as professionals rather than mere salespersons, putting clients' best interests ahead of their own.

sections.heading: 专业人士的四大特征
sections.body: 专业人士是从事特定活动的胜任者,展现四项特征:(1) 掌握专业知识的证据——具备产品/主题的技术知识与技能,这赋予可信度;代表须达到入门学历要求、通过规则与法规、产品知识、营销技巧、销售流程及道德等课程,并每年完成规定时数的持续专业发展(CPD),以紧跟法律变化、新产品与新技术。(2) 可靠性——言出必行、信守承诺,即使在传递坏消息(如投资表现不佳或保费上调)时亦然,并在社交媒体时代及时告知客户程序事项或财务规划报告的进展。(3) 管理自身利益——当客户利益与自身利益冲突时,须让客户利益在合理范围内优先。(4) 对同业的忠诚精神——对整个金融服务业的福祉、健康、地位与声誉负有谨慎义务。

sections.heading: Four characteristics of a professional
sections.body: A professional is a competent person engaged in a specific activity, exhibiting four characteristics: (1) Evidence of mastery of subject matter — technical knowledge and skills give credibility; representatives must meet academic entry requirements, pass courses on rules/regulations, product knowledge, marketing skills, the sales process and ethics, and complete a prescribed number of CPD hours per year to stay abreast of changes in the law, new products and technologies. (2) Reliability — performing what one says and keeping promises, even when delivering bad news (poor investment performance or premium increases), and keeping clients informed of progress in today's social-media world. (3) Management of self-interest — where the client's and the professional's interests conflict, the client's interest must prevail within a reasonable scope. (4) Spirit of loyalty to fellow representatives — a duty of care to the overall well-being, health, status and reputation of the financial services industry.

sections.heading: 管理自身利益的细微之处
sections.body: 专业人士的执业须在商业上可行,因为他对家庭负有财务责任,因此把合理的盈利因素纳入执业是合理的。但细微之处在于:利益冲突时客户利益应在合理范围内优先。专业人士不被要求做免费工作;若客户利益过于压倒性,专业人士应请求被解除对该客户的服务。放弃'纯自私的商业观'回应了只顾自己赚钱、不顾客户需求的推销员心态。追求自利是自然且可接受的,但'纯自私的商业观'鼓励无限度自利乃至自私,在道德上不可接受。专业财务顾问代表(有别于单纯推销员)会以正义、诚信、诚实与公平等道德考量约束自利。

sections.heading: The subtle point on self-interest
sections.body: A professional's practice must be commercially feasible, as he is accountable to his family financially, so it is reasonable to build in a profitability factor while serving the client. The subtle point: on a conflict of interest, the client's interest should prevail within a reasonable scope. A professional is not expected to do free work, and if the client's interest is too overwhelming, he should ask to be discharged. Abandoning the 'strictly selfish commercial view' addresses the salesperson mindset of selling only to make money without regard to the client's needs. Pursuing self-interest is natural and acceptable, but a 'strictly selfish commercial view' pushes self-interest without limit into selfishness, which is ethically unacceptable. A professional representative (unlike a mere salesperson) constrains self-interest by justice, integrity, honesty and fairness.

sections.heading: 构成专业的基本要求
sections.body: 判断 FAA 下的财务咨询业是否为专业,需满足下列要素:学术研习(入门要求与技术知识掌握)、入门门槛(不可自封专业;须通过规定考试并满足诚实、正直、财务健全等要求)、独立性(自行进行事实查找并客观判断)、公众认可(通过会员、认证、发牌、登记等)、管理机关/规管者(监督其行为)、专业团体/行业协会、以及成文的道德守则。新加坡情境下:代表须通过强制考试,细节见 MAS Notice FAA-N26(Notice FAA-N26 Competency Requirements for Representatives of Financial Advisers);MAS 是 FAA 的主管机关;《2001 年财务顾问法》(FAA 2001)是规管金融服务活动行为的主要成文法。

sections.heading: Basic requirements for a profession
sections.body: To assess whether the financial advisory business under the FAA is a profession, the following must be present: academic study (entry requirements and mastery of technical subject matter); entrance barriers (no self-declared professional; passing prescribed examinations and meeting requirements such as honesty, integrity and financial soundness); independence (own fact-find and objective judgment); public recognition (membership, accreditation, licensing, registration); a governing authority/regulator that administers and supervises conduct; professional societies/industry associations; and a code of ethics. In Singapore, a representative must pass a mandatory exam set out in MAS Notice FAA-N26 (Competency Requirements for Representatives of Financial Advisers); the MAS administers the FAA; and the Financial Advisers Act 2001 is the main statute regulating financial services conduct.

sections.heading: 管理机关、协会与公平交易
sections.body: 新加坡没有单一协会代表全体财务顾问代表,但存在数个可信协会代表不同类别代表的声音。专业团体或协会会制定道德守则,为成员设定行为标准。MAS 同时为新加坡所有金融机构发布公平交易(FAIR Dealing)指引。综合各项要素,财务咨询业满足成为'专业'的条件;若答案为肯定,则代表即为专业人士,客户也据此期待其行事。

sections.heading: Governing authority, associations and FAIR dealing
sections.body: There is no single association representing all representatives in Singapore, but several credible associations speak for different categories of representatives. A professional society or association maintains a code of ethics setting standards of conduct for members. The MAS also issues FAIR Dealing guidelines for all financial institutions in Singapore. Taken together, the financial advisory business satisfies the requirements of a profession; if the answer is positive, the representative is a professional and clients expect him to act as one.

sections.heading: 客户对专业行为的十项期望
sections.body: 客户在判断对方是否专业时,至少看十件事:(1) 是否倾听客户、了解其个人与财务信息及需求愿望;(2) 是否回答客户问题(无法即答时不应忽视或敷衍,而应说明需研究并尽快答复,并探询更多问题);(3) 是否搜集足够信息以提供良好建议(彻底的事实查找);(4) 是否就金融产品教育客户(解释关键特征);(5) 是否在推荐复杂高风险产品(如挂钩投资寿险保单 ILP、交易所交易基金 ETF 等指定投资产品)时考虑客户的知识或风险承受能力(进行客户知识评估 CKA 或账户审查);(6) 是否告知客户自身资历;(7) 是否给予客户稳定持续的财务咨询服务(关系非一次性,需定期检视);(8) 是否妥善处理金钱事务;(9) 是否在适当时转介其他专家;(10) 是否对信息显得脱节。

sections.heading: Ten client expectations of professional behaviour
sections.body: A client looks for at least ten things: (1) Does the professional listen and understand the client's needs and aspirations? (2) Does he answer questions (and, if not immediately, say research is needed, reply promptly and probe for more questions rather than gloss over)? (3) Does he gather sufficient information through a thorough fact-find? (4) Does he educate the client about the product's key features? (5) Does he take into account the client's knowledge or ability to handle risk — conducting a Customer Knowledge Assessment or account review for complex, high-risk products such as ILPs and ETFs? (6) Does he tell the client his credentials? (7) Does he give a sense of steady, on-going advisory service (relationships are seldom one-off)? (8) Does he handle money matters properly? (9) Does he refer to other experts where appropriate? (10) Does he seem out of touch with his information?

sections.heading: 妥善处理金钱事务与转介
sections.body: 除非另获特别授权,财务顾问代表一般不应直接向客户收取现金、支票或任何形式的付款。若以现金支付,应缴交至公司注册营业地址的出纳;若以支票支付,应抬头为公司名称并尽快交予公司出纳。此举旨在避免任何混同资金、不当处理金钱或挪用款项的误解、指控或投诉。此外,代表非财务律师、会计师、税务专家或所有金融相关领域的专家,客户理解此点并期待在信托、遗产或税务规划等事项上获得适当转介,以咨询相关专家。

sections.heading: Handling money matters and referrals
sections.body: Unless specifically authorised, a representative should generally not directly collect cash, cheque or any payment from a client. Cash should be paid to the firm's cashier at its registered business address; cheques should be made payable in the company's name and handed to the cashier as soon as possible. This avoids any misunderstanding, allegation or complaint of comingling, mishandling or misappropriation of funds. A representative is not a financial lawyer, accountant, taxation expert or an expert in all financial-related fields; the client understands this and expects appropriate referrals to a relevant specialist for matters such as trust, estate or tax planning.

sections.heading: 财务顾问代表作为专业人士的责任
sections.body: 代表是受 MAS 依 FAA 规管的、为其主事人(法团)从事活动的个人。FAA 第 2(1) 条定义'代表'为受雇于、代理或经安排为财务顾问执行任何财务咨询服务的人,无论是否获酬、报酬以薪金/工资/佣金或其他形式支付,并包括为财务顾问执行此类服务的任何高级人员。FAA 第 27(1) 条规定代表只能为一个主事人行事,或为多于一个但属相关法团的主事人行事,目的是 (a) 确保主事人始终密切监督代表,及 (b) 厘清代表身份及投诉与赔偿的责任归属。虽然业务行为的最终责任在主事人(法团),但代表个人有责任以专业人士而非单纯推销员的标准行事。

sections.heading: Responsibilities as a professional
sections.body: Representatives are individuals engaged in their principals' (corporations') activities, regulated by the MAS under the FAA. Section 2(1) defines 'representative' as a person, by whatever name called, in the direct employment of, acting for, or by arrangement with a financial adviser, who performs any financial advisory service on its behalf, whether or not remunerated and whether by salary, wages, commission or otherwise, and includes any officer performing such service. Section 27(1) provides that a representative acts for only one principal, or for more than one only if they are related corporations — to (a) ensure the principal closely monitors and supervises the representative at all times and (b) secure clarity on status and responsibility for complaints and redress. Although ultimate responsibility for business conduct rests with the principal, the individual representative has a duty to behave like a professional, not a mere salesperson.

sections.heading: 专业三大职责
sections.body: 上述讨论显示,财务顾问代表确实具备专业人士的特征,金融服务业作为一种职业满足被承认为'专业'的要求。但同样重要的是,代表须不负其名并满足客户期望。代表在 FAA 下提供财务咨询服务或推荐投资产品时的专业职责为:(a) 遵守适用法律、规则、法规、通知、通函、咨询、指引及监管机关相关文书的精神与文字;(b) 遵循其所属团体或协会的道德守则或咨询销售流程;(c) 将客户最佳利益置于自身利益之前。

sections.heading: Three professional duties
sections.body: The discussion shows that representatives do have the characteristics of a professional, and the financial services industry satisfies the requirements to be recognised as a profession. Equally important, representatives must live up to the name and clients' expectations. Their professional duties when providing financial advisory services or recommending investment products under the FAA are: (a) to obey the spirit and letter of the applicable law, rules, regulations, notices, circulars, advisories, guidelines and relevant instruments of the regulatory authorities; (b) to follow the code of ethics or advisory sales process of their respective society or association; and (c) to put the best interests of clients ahead of their own.

keyPoints: 四大特征:掌握知识、可靠性、管理自身利益、对同业忠诚 / Four characteristics: mastery, reliability, management of self-interest, loyalty to fellow reps
利益冲突时客户利益在合理范围内优先,但代表无须做免费工作 / On conflict, client's interest prevails within a reasonable scope, but no free work is expected
构成专业八要素:学术、入门门槛、独立、公众认可、管理机关、协会、道德守则 / Profession's requirements: academic study, entrance barriers, independence, public recognition, governing authority, associations, code of ethics
强制考试见 MAS Notice FAA-N26;MAS 主管 FAA;FAA 2001 为主要规管成文法 / Mandatory exam in MAS Notice FAA-N26; MAS administers the FAA; FAA 2001 is the main statute
客户十项期望含倾听、教育、CKA/风险评估、妥善处理金钱、转介专家 / Ten client expectations include listening, educating, CKA/risk assessment, proper money handling, referrals
未经授权不应直接收款;现金/支票交公司出纳,避免混同与挪用指控 / Do not collect payment without authorisation; cash/cheque go to the firm's cashier to avoid comingling allegations
FAA s2(1) 定义代表;s27(1) 一个主事人规则;责任最终在主事人但个人须专业行事 / FAA s2(1) defines representative; s27(1) one-principal rule; ultimate responsibility on principal but individual must act professionally
专业三大职责:守法、遵守道德守则、客户最佳利益优先 / Three duties: obey the law, follow the code of ethics, put clients' best interests first
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.15 — Professionalism (incl. MAS Notice FAA-N26, FAA s2(1), s27(1))
